name: Vitamin D & Bone
description: Common vitamin D synthesis, activation, and receptor markers. Useful for predisposition only; labs still matter most.

snps.description: GWAS marker near DHCR7/NADSYN1 associated with lower circulating 25-hydroxyvitamin D in some populations.

TT.note: No common lower-vitamin-D risk allele detected at this site.

GT.note: One lower-vitamin-D risk allele is present. Sun exposure and season can dominate the actual lab result.

GG.note: Two lower-vitamin-D risk alleles are present. This is a stronger inherited tendency toward lower 25(OH)D.

snps.description: CYP2R1 variant associated with baseline vitamin D level and supplementation response.

AA.note: No common lower-activity CYP2R1 signal detected at this site.

AG.note: One common CYP2R1 risk allele is present. Some people with this pattern run lower 25(OH)D at baseline.

GG.note: Two common CYP2R1 risk alleles are present. This is a stronger genetic tendency toward lower 25(OH)D.

snps.description: GC variant associated with lower circulating 25-hydroxyvitamin D and lower vitamin D binding-protein levels.

TT.note: No common lower-vitamin-D GC risk allele detected at this site.

TG.note: One GC risk allele is present. This can pull average circulating 25(OH)D lower than expected.

GG.note: Two GC risk alleles are present. This is one of the stronger common inherited signals for lower circulating 25(OH)D.

snps.description: Common vitamin D receptor variant already used in the methylation panel; repeated here because it fits vitamin D signaling directly.

AA.note: Lower receptor efficiency — vitamin D signaling may be a bit less resilient.
